log.Fatal / os.Exit called from a non-main constructor or processing function.
This aborts the entire process and bypasses caller error handling - return
an error instead so the caller can recover. log.Fatal is acceptable only
in package main.

mapstructure.Decode silently ignores unknown keys in the input map.
For configuration parsing this hides typos (e.g. csv_pathh instead of
csv_path). Use mapstructure.NewDecoder with a DecoderConfig that sets
ErrorUnused: true so unknown keys produce an error.

Function calls itself recursively after a sleep on the error arm with
no retry counter, no max-attempts, and no context-deadline check.
Each recursion adds a stack frame; under sustained failure the
goroutine eventually stack-overflows. Use a bounded loop or a
backoff library (e.g. cenkalti/backoff) with MaxElapsedTime/Permanent.

Function receives a context.Context parameter but creates a fresh
context.Background() / context.TODO() inside the body and uses that
instead. The caller-supplied ctx (with its deadline and cancellation)
is silently dropped. Pass the parameter ctx downstream, or document
why a fresh context is intentional.
Goroutine spawn requires lifecycle, cancellation, and shutdown ownership evidence.

Goroutine launched without a deferred recover at the top of its body.
A panic inside the goroutine cannot be caught by the launching frame
and will crash the whole process. For fan-out / long-lived / per-
request goroutines, add
`defer func() { if r := recover(); r != nil { log + metric } }()`
at the start of the body.
Channel communication requires blocking, cancellation, and ownership evidence.

Slice is pre-allocated with len() (not cap), filled by indexed
assignment, but the loop body uses continue on error - leaving
zero/nil-valued holes in the result. Downstream consumers that
assume every slot is valid will dereference the nil. Either use
make([]T, 0, len(input)) with append, or resize the slice after
the loop with the count of successful entries.

Assignment mutates http.DefaultClient or http.DefaultTransport from a
library/constructor. These are process-wide globals - mutating them
affects every other consumer in the process, makes tests order-
dependent, and can permanently bind cached env-derived state like
ProxyFromEnvironment. Build a private *http.Client/*http.Transport
and store it on your struct instead.
HTTP client API use requires external-call timeout, cancellation, TLS, and failure-observation evidence.
HTTP client API use requires external-call timeout, cancellation, TLS, and failure-observation evidence.
HTTP request is constructed without context; require documented deadline/cancellation behavior.

cenkalti/backoff ExponentialBackOff treats MaxElapsedTime = 0 as
"retry forever", not "no retry". If unbounded retry is the intent,
add a comment documenting it; otherwise set a finite duration (e.g.
a few minutes) so the process can fail-fast and a supervisor can
restart it.
HTTP server API use requires listener, request-boundary, timeout, and static-file review evidence.

HTTP handler appears to return 200 unconditionally. If this is mounted
as a readiness probe it will report ready even before dependencies are
reachable. Either gate the 200 on a real readiness check, or rename
the route to /livez and add a separate /readyz with a real check.

mapstructure.Decode silently ignores unknown keys in the input map.
For configuration parsing this hides typos (e.g. csv_pathh instead of
csv_path). Use mapstructure.NewDecoder with a DecoderConfig that sets
ErrorUnused: true so unknown keys produce an error.
